onboarding.step4Tip1Body: Pick one habit so easy you can't say no. The smaller the start, the stronger the foundation.
onboarding.step4Tip2Body: Missing once is okay. Missing twice becomes a pattern. Consistency beats intensity every time.
onboarding.step4Tip3Body: Every check-in compounds. In a few weeks you'll look back and be surprised by your progress.
onboarding.step5Title: What area of your life is calling for a little attention?

No habits yet.

settings.title: Settings
settings.strictScoreModeDescription: On: only fully completed habits count. Off: partial progress counts too.

notifications.enableForTest: Enable notifications for Fovere in your system settings to receive test alerts.
notifications.reminderBody: A quick log now keeps you on track. You've got this! 💪
notifications.habitReminderBody: A quick check-in and you're on track. Keep it up! ✨
notifications.breakHabitReminderBody: Stay within your limit today. You're doing great! 🌟
notifications.testNotificationBody: If you see this, notifications are working!

habitReminders.empty: No habits with reminders. Set a reminder when creating or editing a habit.

deletedHabits.title: Deleted Habits
deletedHabits.subtitle: Deleted habits stay here for 30 days, then are removed permanently.

calendar.noHabitsAddOnHome: No habits yet. Add one on the Home tab.

habitDetail.pauseMessage: This will hide "{{name}}" from your Home screen from today. You can resume it anytime from the Paused section on the home screen.
habitDetail.deleteMessage: {{name}} will be removed from your Home screen from today onward, but its past history will stay in Calendar and Analytics.
habitDetail.futureDateNote: You can't add progress for future dates.
habitDetail.ringDragHint: Drag the dot on the ring to set your progress

wizard.habitNameHelper: Give your habit a clear, motivating name.
wizard.frequencyHelper: How often do you want to track this habit?
wizard.iconHelper: Choose an emoji to represent your habit.
wizard.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Run for at least 30 minutes before work
wizard.targetHelper: Set the quantity you want to reach each time.
wizard.reminderHelper: Choose when you want to be reminded.
wizard.descriptionHelper: Add an optional note about this habit. It will appear on the habit detail screen.

legal.privacyIntro: Fovere is developed and operated by me, Franco Galluzzo.
legal.privacyNoData: Fovere does not collect, store, or process any personal data on external servers. All data you enter into the app (such as your habits and usage history) is stored locally on your device and remains under your control. I do not operate a backend server for Fovere, and I do not transmit your data to myself or to any third parties.
legal.privacyNotifications: The app may use platform features such as local notifications to remind you about your habits. These notifications are scheduled and handled by your device; the content of your reminders is not sent to any servers that I control.
legal.privacyNoAnalytics: I do not use analytics, advertising, or tracking technologies in Fovere.
legal.privacyContact: If you have any questions about this policy, you can contact me at:
legal.termsIntro: By using Fovere, you agree to these Terms of Service.
legal.termsAsIs: Fovere is provided by me, Franco Galluzzo, on an "as is" and "as available" basis, without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.
legal.termsResponsibility: You are responsible for any data you enter into the app and for maintaining backups of your device. I am not liable for any loss of data, productivity, or other damages arising from your use of Fovere.
legal.termsUpdates: I may update these terms from time to time. If I make material changes, I will update the "Last updated" date above. Your continued use of Fovere after any changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
legal.termsContact: For questions about these terms, contact me at:

habitsScoring.title: Habits & scoring
habitsScoring.pointsIntro: Your daily score is on a 0–100 scale. The 100 points are shared equally among all your active habits. Each habit's share is its "weight."
habitsScoring.pointsGood: Build habits (good habits): you earn your habit's weight when you complete the habit that day, and 0 if you don't.
habitsScoring.pointsBad: Break habits (bad habits): you earn your habit's weight by default. If you go over your limit on that day, the overflow is penalized and reduces your share (see Penalties below).
habitsScoring.badHabitsIntro: Break habits help you stay within a limit — for example "no more than 2 coffees" or "no smoking."
habitsScoring.badHabitsDaily: Daily limit: you set a maximum per day (e.g. 1 = did not do it, or 3 = up to 3 times). You "complete" the habit when you stay at or under the limit for that day.
habitsScoring.badHabitsWeekly: Weekly limit: you set a maximum for the whole week. Your progress is the sum of what you logged each day in that week. You complete it when the week's total is at or under the limit.
habitsScoring.badHabitsMonthly: Monthly limit: same idea over the calendar month. Progress is the sum of entries in that month; you complete it when the total is at or under the limit.
habitsScoring.badHabitsBoolean: Yes/No break habits: you either did the thing (fail) or didn't (success). No entry or "Okay" counts as success.
habitsScoring.penaltiesIntro: When you exceed your limit on a break habit, only the overflow on that specific day is penalized. Past days are never penalized again.
habitsScoring.penaltiesFormula: For each unit you're over the limit on that day, you lose one unit of your habit's share of the 100 points. Your contribution is never reduced below 0.
habitsScoring.penaltiesExample: Example: if you have 4 habits, each has weight 25. Your "Limit caffeine" habit allows 2 cups; you had 4 today. You get 25 − (2 × 25) = 0 points from that habit for the day (2 overflow units × full weight).
habitsScoring.weeklyHabitsIntro: Weekly habits are goals you want to hit at least once per week (e.g. "Gym 2 times," "Read 3 times").
habitsScoring.weeklyHabitsProgress: Progress is the sum of what you logged on each day of the current week. The habit is completed when that total reaches or exceeds your target (for build) or stays at or under your limit (for break).
habitsScoring.weeklyHabitsReminder: You can set which weekdays to get a reminder (e.g. Mon, Wed, Fri).
habitsScoring.monthlyHabitsIntro: Monthly habits work the same way over the calendar month. Progress is the sum of entries in that month; you complete the habit when the total meets your target (build) or stays within your limit (break).
habitsScoring.dailyHabitsIntro: Daily habits are checked each day. Build: you complete when you reach your target for that day. Break: you complete when you stay at or under your limit for that day.
habitsScoring.homeRingNote: The completion ring on Home counts only daily habits for that day. Weekly and monthly habits are shown in their own sections and in Analytics.
